description: To discover the exact place or position of; to find. 'Wesho' names the finding of position—determining where something is, establishing location.
sound_symbolism: The word finds. 'We' opens with searching awareness, looking begun. Then 'sho' /ʃo/ settles on the spot, location found.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a transitive verb: 'mia ruela wesho.' (I locate the path.) Related to 'lokue' (place)—locating is finding place.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Locating oneself in present moment is practice—knowing where you are before deciding where to go.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Locating was survival skill—finding water, food, shelter, the way home required reading landscape.
